servicePattern.value: Frequent weekday service: fast and semi-fast trains to London King’s Cross; Thameslink trains through London St Pancras and London Bridge towards Gatwick Airport and destinations south of London; Great Northern services north to Cambridge/King’s Lynn and via Hertford North to Moorgate; Thameslink services north to Peterborough; selected LNER and Lumo intercity calls on the East Coast Main Line.
servicePattern.sourceTitle: Official Great Northern/Thameslink, LNER and Lumo timetables
servicePattern.notes: Representative Monday-Friday inter-peak service level, not a guarantee for every clock hour. Destination entries overlap and must not be summed. frequency.value: Approximately 12 total departures per hour in a representative weekday inter-peak hour, comprising multiple London, Hertford Loop, Cambridge/King’s Lynn, Peterborough and selected intercity services.
frequency.sourceTitle: Official Great Northern/Thameslink, LNER and Lumo timetables
frequency.notes: Representative Monday-Friday inter-peak service level, not a guarantee for every clock hour. Destination entries overlap and must not be summed. Engineering work, seasonal amendments and individual train restrictions can alter the service; check the journey planner for the date of travel.

destinations.value: London King's Cross, London St Pancras International, Finsbury Park, Moorgate, Hertford North, London Bridge, Gatwick Airport, Cambridge, King's Lynn and Peterborough, with selected direct LNER/Lumo services to East Coast destinations including York, Leeds, Newcastle and Edinburgh depending on the train.
destinations.sourceTitle: Official Great Northern/Thameslink, LNER and Lumo timetables
destinations.notes: Principal direct destinations only; stopping patterns and intercity calls vary by train.

journeyOpportunities.value: Stevenage provides high-frequency local, regional and intercity rail opportunities, including direct access to central London, Cambridge, Peterborough, the Hertford Loop and selected East Coast Main Line destinations. Thameslink services provide direct cross-London connectivity, while interchange at London, Cambridge and Peterborough broadens onward national connections.
journeyOpportunities.sourceTitle: Official Great Northern/Thameslink, LNER and Lumo timetables
journeyOpportunities.notes: General accessibility wording; always use date-specific journey planning for a proposed trip.

cycleParking.value: 266 cycle-storage spaces; cycle storage is covered by CCTV.

stepFree.value: Step-free category B1: step-free access is available to all platforms, but access may involve long or steep ramps or streets. Lifts are provided.

ticketing.value: Ticket office and ticket machines available. Ticket office: Monday-Thursday 06:15-20:30; Friday 06:15-21:45; Saturday 06:30-21:30; Sunday 08:00-20:15.

waiting.value: Sheltered waiting area and seating available; heated waiting areas are provided on all platforms.
waiting.sourceTitle: National Rail and Great Northern station information

toilets.value: Toilets, accessible toilets, Changing Places toilet and baby-changing facilities available; the main toilets are located within the booking hall.
toilets.sourceTitle: National Rail and Great Northern station information

carParking.value: Station parking: 17 spaces, including 4 accessible spaces; drop-off/pick-up point available.
carParking.notes: This records the parking provision stated on the National Rail station page and does not include any separate town-centre public car parks.

notes.value: CRS code SVG. Address: Stevenage station, Lytton Way, Stevenage, Hertfordshire, SG1 1XT. Bus and taxi interchange available. This OSM feature is the Stevenage interchange/station complex and duplicates National Rail station SVG; it is not a separately served National Rail station.
notes.notes: Duplicate candidate: retain identifiers and route data, but consolidate with the canonical Stevenage (SVG) station record before publishing nearby-station results.

reviewFlags: duplicate_candidate: Same physical National Rail station as Stevenage (CRS SVG).

records.name: Plaistow

frequencyTotal.sourceTitle: TfL station information and Tube map
frequencyTotal.notes: Representative Monday-Friday daytime service derived from the published line pattern. District and Hammersmith & City destination entries overlap and must not be summed. Actual intervals and terminal allocations vary by time of day and disruption.

value.notes: Hammersmith & City line; representative daytime frequency.
District line; representative allocation varies by working timetable.
District line; representative allocation varies by working timetable.
District line; representative allocation varies by working timetable.
Hammersmith & City trains terminate at Barking; all eastbound trains also call there.
District line; representative daytime frequency.

frequencyByDestination.sourceTitle: TfL station information and Tube map
frequencyByDestination.notes: Representative Monday-Friday daytime service derived from the published line pattern. District and Hammersmith & City destination entries overlap and must not be summed. Actual intervals and terminal allocations vary by time of day and disruption.

servicePattern.value: Frequent District and Hammersmith & City line services operate westbound through central London. Eastbound District line trains continue to Upminster, while Hammersmith & City line trains terminate at Barking.
servicePattern.sourceTitle: TfL station information and Tube map

frequency.value: Approximately 36 total train departures per hour in a representative weekday daytime period (about 18 in each direction).
frequency.sourceTitle: TfL station information and Tube map
frequency.notes: Representative Monday-Friday daytime service derived from the published line pattern. District and Hammersmith & City destination entries overlap and must not be summed. Actual intervals and terminal allocations vary by time of day and disruption.

destinations.value: Direct services include Barking, Upminster, Whitechapel, Liverpool Street, King’s Cross St Pancras, Paddington and Hammersmith, plus District line branches to Ealing Broadway, Richmond and Wimbledon.
destinations.sourceTitle: TfL station information and Tube map
destinations.notes: Principal destinations only; not every train serves every western branch.

journeyOpportunities.value: The station provides frequent direct Underground access to east and central London, with interchange available across the wider Tube, DLR, Elizabeth line and National Rail networks at stations including West Ham, Whitechapel, Liverpool Street, King’s Cross St Pancras and Paddington.
journeyOpportunities.sourceTitle: TfL station information and Tube map

cycleParking.value: TfL does not list cycle parking on the station page.
cycleParking.notes: Absence from TfL's facility list is not treated as proof that cycle parking is unavailable; confirm locally if material to the assessment.

stepFree.value: Not shown as step-free on TfL’s current step-free network guide. Step-free access should therefore not be assumed.
stepFree.sourceTitle: TfL step-free guide and TfL station-accessibility FOI response
stepFree.notes: TfL lists the station among candidates considered for a future step-free upgrade; this does not constitute a committed scheme.

ticketing.value: Ticket hall, ticket gates and self-service ticket machines; Oyster and contactless payment are supported.
ticketing.sourceTitle: TfL station information and London Underground Accessible Travel Policy

waiting.value: TfL does not list a dedicated waiting room on the station page.
waiting.notes: Absence from TfL's facility list is not treated as proof that a dedicated waiting room is unavailable; confirm locally if material to the assessment.

toilets.value: TfL does not list toilets on the station page.
toilets.notes: Absence from TfL's facility list is not treated as proof that toilets is unavailable; confirm locally if material to the assessment.

carParking.value: TfL does not list station car parking on the station page.
carParking.notes: Absence from TfL's facility list is not treated as proof that station car parking is unavailable; confirm locally if material to the assessment.

notes.value: Address: Plaistow Station, Plaistow Road, London E13 0DY. Fare Zone 3. Station facilities listed by TfL include a ticket hall, gates, payphones and Wi-Fi.

records.name: Upton Park

frequencyTotal.sourceTitle: TfL station information and Tube map
frequencyTotal.notes: Representative Monday-Friday daytime service derived from the published line pattern. District and Hammersmith & City destination entries overlap and must not be summed. Actual intervals and terminal allocations vary by time of day and disruption.

value.notes: Hammersmith & City line; representative daytime frequency.
District line; representative allocation varies by working timetable.
District line; representative allocation varies by working timetable.
District line; representative allocation varies by working timetable.
Hammersmith & City trains terminate at Barking; all eastbound trains also call there.
District line; representative daytime frequency.

frequencyByDestination.sourceTitle: TfL station information and Tube map
frequencyByDestination.notes: Representative Monday-Friday daytime service derived from the published line pattern. District and Hammersmith & City destination entries overlap and must not be summed. Actual intervals and terminal allocations vary by time of day and disruption.

servicePattern.value: Frequent District and Hammersmith & City line services operate westbound through central London. Eastbound District line trains continue to Upminster, while Hammersmith & City line trains terminate at Barking.
servicePattern.sourceTitle: TfL station information and Tube map

frequency.value: Approximately 36 total train departures per hour in a representative weekday daytime period (about 18 in each direction).
frequency.sourceTitle: TfL station information and Tube map
frequency.notes: Representative Monday-Friday daytime service derived from the published line pattern. District and Hammersmith & City destination entries overlap and must not be summed. Actual intervals and terminal allocations vary by time of day and disruption.

destinations.value: Direct services include Barking, Upminster, Whitechapel, Liverpool Street, King’s Cross St Pancras, Paddington and Hammersmith, plus District line branches to Ealing Broadway, Richmond and Wimbledon.
destinations.sourceTitle: TfL station information and Tube map
destinations.notes: Principal destinations only; not every train serves every western branch.

journeyOpportunities.value: The station provides frequent direct Underground access to east and central London, with interchange available across the wider Tube, DLR, Elizabeth line and National Rail networks at stations including West Ham, Whitechapel, Liverpool Street, King’s Cross St Pancras and Paddington.
journeyOpportunities.sourceTitle: TfL station information and Tube map

cycleParking.value: TfL does not list cycle parking on the station page.
cycleParking.notes: Absence from TfL's facility list is not treated as proof that cycle parking is unavailable; confirm locally if material to the assessment.

stepFree.value: Not shown as step-free on TfL’s current step-free network guide. Step-free access should therefore not be assumed.
stepFree.sourceTitle: TfL step-free guide and TfL station-accessibility FOI response
stepFree.notes: TfL lists the station among candidates considered for a future step-free upgrade; this does not constitute a committed scheme.

ticketing.value: Ticket hall, ticket gates and self-service ticket machines; Oyster and contactless payment are supported.
ticketing.sourceTitle: TfL station information and London Underground Accessible Travel Policy

waiting.value: TfL does not list a dedicated waiting room on the station page.
waiting.notes: Absence from TfL's facility list is not treated as proof that a dedicated waiting room is unavailable; confirm locally if material to the assessment.

toilets.value: TfL does not list toilets on the station page.
toilets.notes: Absence from TfL's facility list is not treated as proof that toilets is unavailable; confirm locally if material to the assessment.

carParking.value: TfL does not list station car parking on the station page.
carParking.notes: Absence from TfL's facility list is not treated as proof that station car parking is unavailable; confirm locally if material to the assessment.

notes.value: Address: Upton Park Station, Green Street, London E13 9AP. Fare Zone 3. Station facilities listed by TfL include a ticket hall, gates, payphones and Wi-Fi.

frequency.value: Approximately 12 total departures per hour during a typical weekday daytime period, comprising about 6 trains per hour in each direction.
frequency.sourceTitle: TfL DLR timetable and route map

destinations.sourceTitle: TfL DLR station information, timetable and route map

journeyOpportunities.value: West Ham is a major interchange. In addition to the DLR, passengers can interchange with the Jubilee, District and Hammersmith & City lines and c2c National Rail services, providing direct access towards central London, Stratford, Canary Wharf, Barking and South Essex.
journeyOpportunities.notes: The frequency fields in this record count DLR departures only, in line with the record mode.

cycleParking.value: TfL does not list cycle parking on the station page.
cycleParking.notes: Absence from TfL's facility list is not treated as proof that cycle parking is unavailable; confirm locally if material to the assessment.

stepFree.value: Step-free access from street to train. TfL confirms that all DLR stations are step-free.
stepFree.sourceTitle: TfL step-free access information and DLR route map

ticketing.value: Oyster and contactless accepted. A ticket machine is available for Oyster top-up and season-ticket products; passengers should touch in and out.
ticketing.sourceTitle: TfL Types of Tickets and Ways to Pay

waiting.value: Ticket hall and interchange circulation areas are provided; TfL does not list a dedicated waiting room.

toilets.value: TfL does not list toilets on the station page.
toilets.notes: Absence from TfL's facility list is not treated as proof that toilets is unavailable; confirm locally if material to the assessment.

carParking.value: TfL does not list station car parking on the station page.
carParking.notes: Absence from TfL's facility list is not treated as proof that station car parking is unavailable; confirm locally if material to the assessment.

notes.value: TfL stop identifier 940GZZDLWHM; Fare Zone 2/3. Interchange with Jubilee, District and Hammersmith & City lines and c2c National Rail. TfL lists ticket halls, boarding ramps, lifts, escalators, bridge, gates, payphones and Wi-Fi.
